pr.title: Use `Unknown` in `configure.py` until status is confirmed
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: PR 1146 made a semantic-only status cleanup in `configure.py`: it introduced an `Unknown = False` status for objects whose matching status had not yet been confirmed, documented it as "Unconfirmed, might link as-is or with minor changes", and reserved `NonMatching = False` for files that are "Definitely not matching yet". Hundreds of object entries across Melee, runtime, Dolphin, and sysdolphin library lists were changed from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Unknown, ...)` without changing build behavior, because both constants evaluate to `False`.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; added the `Unknown` status constant and reclassified many object entries from `NonMatching` to `Unknown`.

what_changed: Added `Unknown = False` immediately after `Matching = True` in `configure.py`.
Added comments distinguishing `Unknown` from `NonMatching`: `Unknown` means unconfirmed and may link as-is or with minor changes; `NonMatching` means definitely not matching yet.
Changed many `Object(NonMatching, "...")` entries to `Object(Unknown, "...")` in the central library/object configuration.
Kept the effective boolean behavior unchanged because both `Unknown` and `NonMatching` are set to `False`.

The affected object lists span many configured libraries, including examples from `lb`, `cm`, `pl`, `mp`, `ef`, `ft`, `gm`, `gr`, `db`, `mn`, `it`, `if`, `un`, `ty`, `vi`, runtime libraries, Dolphin libraries, and sysdolphin.
smart_moves: Separated an epistemic state from a build state: objects can remain disabled for matching purposes while being labeled as merely unconfirmed instead of known nonmatching.
Preserved build behavior by making `Unknown` another `False` status, avoiding risk while improving metadata accuracy.
Added short inline comments at the status definitions, making the intended workflow visible to future contributors editing `configure.py`.
Avoided prematurely declaring untested files as definitely nonmatching, which keeps future matching triage more accurate.
decomp_lessons: Use distinct status names for different kinds of uncertainty. A file that has not been tested should not be marked the same way as a file proven not to match.
When changing project-wide object metadata, keep the build semantics stable unless the PR is explicitly intended to alter linking or matching behavior.
Centralized status constants in `configure.py` are useful for large-scale triage: future work can promote individual entries from `Unknown` to `Matching` or `NonMatching` after verification.
For decomp progress tracking, semantic labels matter even when the underlying boolean value is identical.
naming_conventions: `Matching = True` marks objects treated as matching.
`Unknown = False` marks objects whose matching status is unconfirmed.
`NonMatching = False` is reserved for objects definitely known not to match yet.
Object entries follow the existing form `Object(StatusConstant, "path/to/source.c")`.
The PR uses capitalized status constants (`Matching`, `Unknown`, `NonMatching`) rather than raw booleans in object lists.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic is evidenced in this PR.
The relevant matching tactic is process-oriented: keep unverified objects as `Unknown` until they are tested, then move them to `Matching` or `NonMatching` based on evidence.
Because `Unknown` and `NonMatching` both evaluate to `False`, this tactic improves status tracking without changing link/build behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that changed `Object(Unknown, ...)` entries to `Object(Matching, ...)` after confirming matches.
Find later PRs that changed `Object(Unknown, ...)` entries to `Object(NonMatching, ...)` after confirming nonmatches.
Search for any tooling or progress-report code that treats `Unknown` differently from `NonMatching` despite both being false.
Audit remaining `Unknown` objects in `configure.py` to identify untested files that may now match.
